mainEntity.name: Can my boss fire me for complaining about chemical fumes making me sick?

acceptedAnswer.text: No, retaliation for filing a claim or reporting safety hazards is illegal. If you are terminated or demoted after reporting respiratory issues, you may have grounds for a separate retaliation lawsuit alongside your workers\' compensation claim.
acceptedAnswer.description: It is illegal for an employer in Utah or Idaho to fire you specifically for filing a workers\' compensation claim or reporting an unsafe breathing environment. However, many employers will use "at-will" employment or "performance issues" as a cover for retaliation. If you were fired shortly after complaining about fumes or asking for a respirator, you may have a secondary claim for wrongful termination or a "retaliatory discharge" lawsuit. It is vital to keep a paper trail of your complaints and the employer\'s response. If you feel you are being pushed out, do not quit—this can forfeit your benefits. Speaking with an attorney immediately can help protect your employment status while your claim is pending.
